claim: US President Donald Trump extended a ceasefire with Iran indefinitely on April 21, 2026, conditional on Iran submitting a diplomatic proposal.
The US maintains a naval blockade on Iranian ports and the Strait of Hormuz as of April 22, 2026.
IRGC spokesperson Ibrahim Zulfagari stated on April 21 that forces are at '100% readiness with fingers on the trigger' for immediate strikes on predetermined targets if aggressed.
Tasnim News released an IRGC target list on April 21 including Saudi Arabia's Yanboo pipeline as a Hormuz alternative.
Iranian Navy protected the oil tanker Sili City through the US blockade into Iranian waters on April 21-22, 2026.
Pakistan mediated the ceasefire extension between the US and Iran, requesting additional time for diplomatic negotiations.
On April 18, 2026, Tehran's Revolutionary Guard fired on a tanker in the Strait of Hormuz and reimposed its blockade after announcing reopening the waterway one day prior.
Iran's economy is losing approximately $500 million daily under combined pressure from war, sanctions, US blockade, and Tehran's disruption of the Strait of Hormuz.
source: Expert analysis cited in search results

claim: Millions of Iranians are feared to be unemployed as devastating war compounds existing crises and accelerates economic collapse.
Iran is experiencing severe shortages of key petrochemical products after recent strikes on main production hubs.
One hundred days after protests erupted across Iran in January 2026, the events represent one of the most widespread and violent protest waves the country has ever faced.
Many Iranians now fear silence more than they fear protest, reflecting the intensity of domestic unrest as of April 2026.

Middle East expert Simon Mabon stated that Iran's dramatic reversal on reopening the Strait of Hormuz reflects strategic positioning by all sides to strengthen their negotiating positions ahead of diplomatic initiatives in Islamabad.
Trump stated the ceasefire extension holds until Iran proposes terms, warning of abrupt end to hostilities otherwise.
The US continues 'maximum pressure' on Iran's finances and energy via Treasury actions as of April 22, 2026.
Major General Abdullahi reiterated high alert status for Iranian forces amid diplomatic suspense.
The original ceasefire between the US and Iran was set to expire on April 22, 2026.
